approval_note: These objectives define instructional infrastructure only. They do not approve lesson drafting or production content.

planned_lessons.title: What Makes Work a Project?

learning_objectives.statement: Distinguish project work from operations using temporary endeavor, uniqueness, and value criteria.
Classify a work request as project, program component, product work, or operations and justify the classification.

expected_competencies: Distinguish project work from operations using temporary endeavor, uniqueness, and value criteria.
Classify a work request as project, program component, product work, or operations and justify the classification.

planned_lessons.title: Value, Benefits, and Project Success

learning_objectives.statement: Explain how outputs, outcomes, benefits, and value differ.
Select success measures that connect project delivery to intended benefits.

expected_competencies: Explain how outputs, outcomes, benefits, and value differ.
Select success measures that connect project delivery to intended benefits.

planned_lessons.title: Project vs. Program vs. Portfolio vs. Operations

learning_objectives.statement: Distinguish projects, programs, portfolios, and operations by purpose, coordination, and decision horizon.
Choose the correct management context for a scenario involving related work or strategic investment.

expected_competencies: Distinguish projects, programs, portfolios, and operations by purpose, coordination, and decision horizon.
Choose the correct management context for a scenario involving related work or strategic investment.

planned_lessons.title: Projects as Vehicles for Change

learning_objectives.statement: Explain how projects enable organizational change and adoption.
Identify change outcomes and transition considerations that belong in project planning.

expected_competencies: Explain how projects enable organizational change and adoption.
Identify change outcomes and transition considerations that belong in project planning.

planned_lessons.title: Phases, Deliverables, and Acceptance

learning_objectives.statement: Differentiate phases, deliverables, acceptance, transition, and formal closure.
Determine when phase-gate or acceptance evidence is required before work advances.

expected_competencies: Differentiate phases, deliverables, acceptance, transition, and formal closure.
Determine when phase-gate or acceptance evidence is required before work advances.

planned_lessons.title: Project vs. Product Management

learning_objectives.statement: Distinguish project management from product management responsibilities and time horizons.
Identify when product decisions and project delivery decisions must be coordinated.

expected_competencies: Distinguish project management from product management responsibilities and time horizons.
Identify when product decisions and project delivery decisions must be coordinated.

planned_lessons.title: Risks, Issues, Assumptions, and Constraints

learning_objectives.statement: Differentiate risks, issues, assumptions, and constraints.
Classify a scenario signal and select the appropriate record or response path.

expected_competencies: Differentiate risks, issues, assumptions, and constraints.
Classify a scenario signal and select the appropriate record or response path.

planned_lessons.title: Leadership vs. Management

learning_objectives.statement: Compare leadership and management behaviors in project contexts.
Select a leadership or management response appropriate to team need and delivery conditions.

expected_competencies: Compare leadership and management behaviors in project contexts.
Select a leadership or management response appropriate to team need and delivery conditions.

planned_lessons.title: PMOs and Organizational Structures

learning_objectives.statement: Compare PMO types and organizational structures by authority, resource access, and governance role.
Predict how organizational structure affects the project manager's authority and escalation path.

expected_competencies: Compare PMO types and organizational structures by authority, resource access, and governance role.
Predict how organizational structure affects the project manager's authority and escalation path.

planned_lessons.title: The PMP Decision-Making Mindset

learning_objectives.statement: Apply value, accountability, collaboration, and proactive problem solving to PMP-style decisions.
Reject common exam-response traps such as premature escalation, avoidance, and unapproved action.

expected_competencies: Apply value, accountability, collaboration, and proactive problem solving to PMP-style decisions.
Reject common exam-response traps such as premature escalation, avoidance, and unapproved action.

planned_lessons.title: Holistic and Systems Thinking

learning_objectives.statement: Explain how project decisions interact across organizational and delivery systems.
Analyze a local decision for downstream effects on value, risk, quality, and stakeholders.

expected_competencies: Explain how project decisions interact across organizational and delivery systems.
Analyze a local decision for downstream effects on value, risk, quality, and stakeholders.

planned_lessons.title: Value-Driven Delivery

learning_objectives.statement: Prioritize work and decisions by value rather than output volume.
Select feedback and measurement practices that protect expected outcomes and benefits.

expected_competencies: Prioritize work and decisions by value rather than output volume.
Select feedback and measurement practices that protect expected outcomes and benefits.

planned_lessons.title: Building Quality In

learning_objectives.statement: Differentiate prevention, assurance, control, verification, and acceptance.
Choose an action that builds quality into the process instead of relying on inspection alone.

expected_competencies: Differentiate prevention, assurance, control, verification, and acceptance.
Choose an action that builds quality into the process instead of relying on inspection alone.

planned_lessons.title: Accountability and Ethical Leadership

learning_objectives.statement: Apply accountability, ethics, transparency, and stewardship to project decisions.
Identify when a leader must disclose, escalate, or decline an action to protect stakeholders and integrity.

expected_competencies: Apply accountability, ethics, transparency, and stewardship to project decisions.
Identify when a leader must disclose, escalate, or decline an action to protect stakeholders and integrity.

planned_lessons.title: Empowered Teams and Culture

learning_objectives.statement: Explain how empowerment, psychological safety, and clear boundaries support performance.
Choose team practices that distribute decisions without abandoning accountability.

expected_competencies: Explain how empowerment, psychological safety, and clear boundaries support performance.
Choose team practices that distribute decisions without abandoning accountability.

planned_lessons.title: Sustainability Across the Project

learning_objectives.statement: Identify environmental, social, economic, and operational sustainability considerations.
Evaluate a project decision for lifecycle impacts and stakeholder tradeoffs.

expected_competencies: Identify environmental, social, economic, and operational sustainability considerations.
Evaluate a project decision for lifecycle impacts and stakeholder tradeoffs.

planned_lessons.title: Project Life Cycles and Phase Gates

learning_objectives.statement: Differentiate project life cycle, development approach, phase, iteration, and phase gate.
Select a life-cycle structure that fits uncertainty, governance, and delivery needs.

expected_competencies: Differentiate project life cycle, development approach, phase, iteration, and phase gate.
Select a life-cycle structure that fits uncertainty, governance, and delivery needs.

planned_lessons.title: Predictive Delivery

learning_objectives.statement: Explain the planning, baseline, control, and handoff characteristics of predictive delivery.
Identify when predictive practices are appropriate and how change should be governed.

expected_competencies: Explain the planning, baseline, control, and handoff characteristics of predictive delivery.
Identify when predictive practices are appropriate and how change should be governed.

planned_lessons.title: Iterative and Incremental Delivery

learning_objectives.statement: Distinguish iterative learning from incremental delivery.
Choose iterative, incremental, or combined patterns for a given uncertainty and feedback need.

expected_competencies: Distinguish iterative learning from incremental delivery.
Choose iterative, incremental, or combined patterns for a given uncertainty and feedback need.

planned_lessons.title: Adaptive Delivery

learning_objectives.statement: Explain adaptive planning, short feedback loops, and evolving scope.
Select adaptive practices that preserve value, transparency, and stakeholder learning.

expected_competencies: Explain adaptive planning, short feedback loops, and evolving scope.
Select adaptive practices that preserve value, transparency, and stakeholder learning.

planned_lessons.title: Hybrid Delivery

learning_objectives.statement: Explain why hybrid delivery combines practices rather than merely mixing terminology.
Identify which components should use predictive or adaptive controls in a hybrid scenario.

expected_competencies: Explain why hybrid delivery combines practices rather than merely mixing terminology.
Identify which components should use predictive or adaptive controls in a hybrid scenario.

planned_lessons.title: Tailoring the Approach

learning_objectives.statement: Evaluate context factors that drive tailoring decisions.
Justify a tailored approach while preserving required governance, quality, and compliance outcomes.

expected_competencies: Evaluate context factors that drive tailoring decisions.
Justify a tailored approach while preserving required governance, quality, and compliance outcomes.

planned_lessons.title: PMBOK 8 Domains and Focus Areas

learning_objectives.statement: Explain how PMBOK domains, focus areas, processes, and principles relate.
Navigate the structural model to locate the right process or domain for a project need.

expected_competencies: Explain how PMBOK domains, focus areas, processes, and principles relate.
Navigate the structural model to locate the right process or domain for a project need.

planned_lessons.title: Charter, Business Case, and Benefits Plan

learning_objectives.statement: Differentiate the business case, charter, benefits plan, and related authorization artifacts.
Select the artifact or decision authority needed to initiate or justify work.

expected_competencies: Differentiate the business case, charter, benefits plan, and related authorization artifacts.
Select the artifact or decision authority needed to initiate or justify work.

planned_lessons.title: Integrated Project Management Plan

learning_objectives.statement: Explain how subsidiary plans and baselines form an integrated project management plan.
Identify conflicts and dependencies that must be reconciled across component plans.

expected_competencies: Explain how subsidiary plans and baselines form an integrated project management plan.
Identify conflicts and dependencies that must be reconciled across component plans.

planned_lessons.title: Project Execution and Knowledge

learning_objectives.statement: Differentiate project execution, knowledge creation, and knowledge transfer.
Select practices that capture and reuse explicit and tacit knowledge during delivery.

expected_competencies: Differentiate project execution, knowledge creation, and knowledge transfer.
Select practices that capture and reuse explicit and tacit knowledge during delivery.

planned_lessons.title: Monitoring Performance and Managing Change

learning_objectives.statement: Trace work performance data through analysis, reporting, and change decisions.
Choose the correct monitoring or change-control response to a performance signal.

expected_competencies: Trace work performance data through analysis, reporting, and change decisions.
Choose the correct monitoring or change-control response to a performance signal.

planned_lessons.title: Project and Phase Closure

learning_objectives.statement: Explain administrative, contractual, financial, knowledge, and transition activities in closure.
Determine whether evidence supports formal project or phase closure.

expected_competencies: Explain administrative, contractual, financial, knowledge, and transition activities in closure.
Determine whether evidence supports formal project or phase closure.

planned_lessons.title: Procurement, Contracts, and Sourcing

learning_objectives.statement: Compare make-or-buy, sourcing, contract risk allocation, selection, and administration decisions.
Choose a procurement approach or contract family that fits uncertainty and risk ownership.

expected_competencies: Compare make-or-buy, sourcing, contract risk allocation, selection, and administration decisions.
Choose a procurement approach or contract family that fits uncertainty and risk ownership.

planned_lessons.title: Requirements Elicitation and Analysis

learning_objectives.statement: Select elicitation and analysis techniques appropriate to stakeholder and solution context.
Evaluate requirements for clarity, traceability, feasibility, and value.

expected_competencies: Select elicitation and analysis techniques appropriate to stakeholder and solution context.
Evaluate requirements for clarity, traceability, feasibility, and value.

planned_lessons.title: Product Scope vs. Project Scope

learning_objectives.statement: Distinguish product scope from project scope and explain their interaction.
Classify a requested change by the scope boundary it affects.

expected_competencies: Distinguish product scope from project scope and explain their interaction.
Classify a requested change by the scope boundary it affects.

planned_lessons.title: Scope Statements, WBS, and Baselines

learning_objectives.statement: Explain how scope statements, WBS elements, work packages, and the scope baseline relate.
Decompose approved scope into a structure that supports planning and control.

expected_competencies: Explain how scope statements, WBS elements, work packages, and the scope baseline relate.
Decompose approved scope into a structure that supports planning and control.

planned_lessons.title: Scope Validation and Control

learning_objectives.statement: Differentiate scope validation from scope control and quality control.
Select the correct acceptance or change response for a scope scenario.

expected_competencies: Differentiate scope validation from scope control and quality control.
Select the correct acceptance or change response for a scope scenario.

planned_lessons.title: Quality Planning, Assurance, and Control

learning_objectives.statement: Distinguish quality planning, assurance, control, verification, and acceptance.
Choose quality practices and evidence appropriate to prevention, process improvement, or deliverable evaluation.

expected_competencies: Distinguish quality planning, assurance, control, verification, and acceptance.
Choose quality practices and evidence appropriate to prevention, process improvement, or deliverable evaluation.

planned_lessons.title: Building the Project Schedule

learning_objectives.statement: Sequence activities, estimate durations, and develop a logically coherent schedule model.
Evaluate schedule assumptions, dependencies, calendars, and resource effects.

expected_competencies: Sequence activities, estimate durations, and develop a logically coherent schedule model.
Evaluate schedule assumptions, dependencies, calendars, and resource effects.

planned_lessons.title: Critical Path, Float, and Compression

learning_objectives.statement: Calculate and interpret critical path, total float, and schedule compression tradeoffs.
Select crashing, fast tracking, or no compression based on risk and constraints.

expected_competencies: Calculate and interpret critical path, total float, and schedule compression tradeoffs.
Select crashing, fast tracking, or no compression based on risk and constraints.

planned_lessons.title: Cost Estimating and Budgeting

learning_objectives.statement: Explain estimating, reserves, funding, and budget-baseline development.
Select an estimating or budgeting response appropriate to available information and uncertainty.

expected_competencies: Explain estimating, reserves, funding, and budget-baseline development.
Select an estimating or budgeting response appropriate to available information and uncertainty.

planned_lessons.title: Earned Value Interpretation

learning_objectives.statement: Calculate and interpret core earned value indices and forecasts.
Use EVM results to diagnose performance and recommend a proportional response.

expected_competencies: Calculate and interpret core earned value indices and forecasts.
Use EVM results to diagnose performance and recommend a proportional response.

planned_lessons.title: Financial Selection Metrics and Forecasting

learning_objectives.statement: Interpret ROI, NPV, payback, IRR, and forecast measures for project decisions.
Compare financial alternatives without treating a single metric as the only decision criterion.

expected_competencies: Interpret ROI, NPV, payback, IRR, and forecast measures for project decisions.
Compare financial alternatives without treating a single metric as the only decision criterion.

planned_lessons.title: Identifying and Analyzing Stakeholders

learning_objectives.statement: Identify stakeholders and apply appropriate analysis models.
Prioritize engagement effort using influence, interest, impact, urgency, and legitimacy.

expected_competencies: Identify stakeholders and apply appropriate analysis models.
Prioritize engagement effort using influence, interest, impact, urgency, and legitimacy.

planned_lessons.title: Engagement and Communications

learning_objectives.statement: Select communication methods, channels, and engagement strategies for stakeholder needs.
Evaluate communication effectiveness and adjust the plan based on feedback and context.

expected_competencies: Select communication methods, channels, and engagement strategies for stakeholder needs.
Evaluate communication effectiveness and adjust the plan based on feedback and context.

planned_lessons.title: Resource Planning and Acquisition

learning_objectives.statement: Plan, estimate, acquire, develop, and control physical and team resources.
Resolve resource constraints using priorities, calendars, negotiation, and governance.

expected_competencies: Plan, estimate, acquire, develop, and control physical and team resources.
Resolve resource constraints using priorities, calendars, negotiation, and governance.

planned_lessons.title: Team Leadership and Development

learning_objectives.statement: Choose leadership, motivation, coaching, and team-development practices for team maturity and context.
Diagnose team-performance barriers and select a development response.

expected_competencies: Choose leadership, motivation, coaching, and team-development practices for team maturity and context.
Diagnose team-performance barriers and select a development response.

planned_lessons.title: Conflict Resolution and Negotiation

learning_objectives.statement: Compare conflict-resolution and negotiation approaches.
Select and sequence a response that addresses interests, relationships, authority, and urgency.

expected_competencies: Compare conflict-resolution and negotiation approaches.
Select and sequence a response that addresses interests, relationships, authority, and urgency.

planned_lessons.title: Risk Planning and Identification

learning_objectives.statement: Plan risk management and identify threats, opportunities, causes, and effects.
Write and classify a risk statement with ownership and traceability.

expected_competencies: Plan risk management and identify threats, opportunities, causes, and effects.
Write and classify a risk statement with ownership and traceability.

planned_lessons.title: Risk Analysis and Response Strategies

learning_objectives.statement: Select qualitative or quantitative analysis and appropriate threat or opportunity responses.
Evaluate residual, secondary, and overall project risk after response planning.

expected_competencies: Select qualitative or quantitative analysis and appropriate threat or opportunity responses.
Evaluate residual, secondary, and overall project risk after response planning.

planned_lessons.title: Monitoring Risks and Managing Issues

learning_objectives.statement: Differentiate risk monitoring, issue management, escalation, and response implementation.
Choose evidence and actions needed to keep risk and issue records current.

expected_competencies: Differentiate risk monitoring, issue management, escalation, and response implementation.
Choose evidence and actions needed to keep risk and issue records current.

planned_lessons.title: Agile Values, Principles, and Mindset

learning_objectives.statement: Explain agile values, principles, mindset, and empirical learning.
Select behavior consistent with adaptive value delivery rather than ceremonial compliance.

expected_competencies: Explain agile values, principles, mindset, and empirical learning.
Select behavior consistent with adaptive value delivery rather than ceremonial compliance.

planned_lessons.title: Scrum Roles, Events, and Artifacts

learning_objectives.statement: Distinguish Scrum accountabilities, events, artifacts, and commitments.
Diagnose a Scrum scenario and identify the appropriate accountability or event outcome.

expected_competencies: Distinguish Scrum accountabilities, events, artifacts, and commitments.
Diagnose a Scrum scenario and identify the appropriate accountability or event outcome.

planned_lessons.title: Kanban, Lean, and Flow

learning_objectives.statement: Explain Lean principles, Kanban practices, WIP limits, and flow measures.
Use flow signals to identify bottlenecks and select an improvement experiment.

expected_competencies: Explain Lean principles, Kanban practices, WIP limits, and flow measures.
Use flow signals to identify bottlenecks and select an improvement experiment.

planned_lessons.title: Product Vision, Backlogs, and User Stories

learning_objectives.statement: Connect product vision, roadmap, backlog, epics, features, and user stories.
Evaluate backlog items for clarity, value, readiness, and traceability.

expected_competencies: Connect product vision, roadmap, backlog, epics, features, and user stories.
Evaluate backlog items for clarity, value, readiness, and traceability.

planned_lessons.title: Agile Estimation and Forecasting

learning_objectives.statement: Compare relative estimation, velocity, throughput, lead time, and cycle time.
Select and interpret an adaptive forecast without treating estimates as guarantees.

expected_competencies: Compare relative estimation, velocity, throughput, lead time, and cycle time.
Select and interpret an adaptive forecast without treating estimates as guarantees.

planned_lessons.title: MVP, MMF, and Early Value

learning_objectives.statement: Distinguish MVP, MMF, prototype, increment, and release concepts.
Choose the smallest value-bearing delivery strategy that supports learning or benefit realization.

expected_competencies: Distinguish MVP, MMF, prototype, increment, and release concepts.
Choose the smallest value-bearing delivery strategy that supports learning or benefit realization.

planned_lessons.title: Servant Leadership and Self-Organizing Teams

learning_objectives.statement: Apply servant leadership and self-organization within clear goals and constraints.
Identify when to coach, facilitate, remove impediments, or allow the team to decide.

expected_competencies: Apply servant leadership and self-organization within clear goals and constraints.
Identify when to coach, facilitate, remove impediments, or allow the team to decide.

planned_lessons.title: Hybrid Integration

learning_objectives.statement: Design a coherent hybrid operating model with explicit interfaces and controls.
Analyze hybrid failure modes caused by conflicting cadences, roles, artifacts, or governance.

expected_competencies: Design a coherent hybrid operating model with explicit interfaces and controls.
Analyze hybrid failure modes caused by conflicting cadences, roles, artifacts, or governance.

planned_lessons.title: AI Foundations for Project Managers

learning_objectives.statement: Explain AI, machine learning, generative AI, and automation at a project-manager level.
Identify appropriate and inappropriate expectations for AI-supported project work.

expected_competencies: Explain AI, machine learning, generative AI, and automation at a project-manager level.
Identify appropriate and inappropriate expectations for AI-supported project work.

planned_lessons.title: Automation, Assistance, and Augmentation

learning_objectives.statement: Differentiate automation, assistance, and augmentation by human involvement and decision authority.
Select an adoption level that fits task risk, data quality, and oversight needs.

expected_competencies: Differentiate automation, assistance, and augmentation by human involvement and decision authority.
Select an adoption level that fits task risk, data quality, and oversight needs.

planned_lessons.title: Responsible AI and Human Accountability

learning_objectives.statement: Apply privacy, bias, transparency, safety, copyright, and human-accountability controls to AI use.
Evaluate an AI use case and specify review, escalation, and documentation safeguards.

expected_competencies: Apply privacy, bias, transparency, safety, copyright, and human-accountability controls to AI use.
Evaluate an AI use case and specify review, escalation, and documentation safeguards.

planned_lessons.title: AI Use Cases Across the Project Life Cycle

learning_objectives.statement: Identify AI-supported use cases across initiation, planning, execution, monitoring, and closure.
Choose an AI-assisted workflow while preserving source validation, decision ownership, and auditability.

expected_competencies: Identify AI-supported use cases across initiation, planning, execution, monitoring, and closure.
Choose an AI-assisted workflow while preserving source validation, decision ownership, and auditability.
